items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 16th, 2025 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9494, (Mon, Jun 16th)
More Steganography!, (Sat, Jun 14th)
items.content: <article>I spotted another interesting file that uses, once again, steganography. It seems to be a trend (see one of my previous diaries[1]). The file is an malicious Excel sheet called blcopy.xls. Office documents are rare these days because Microsoft improved the rules to allow automatic macro execution[2]. But it does not mean that Office documents can't execute malicious code. In the sample I found (SHA256:c92c761a4c5c3f44e914d6654a678953d56d4d3a2329433afe1710b59c9acd3a), there are other embedded XLS sheets:</article>

items.title: Serverless Tokens in the Cloud: Exploitation and Detections
items.author: Zohar Zigdon
items.content: Understand the mechanics of serverless authentication: three simulated attacks across major CSPs offer effective approaches for application developers.
